paper_title: Materials and Methods for Cement and Diatomite Blending

methods.description: Process for preparing diatomite powder from raw diatomite by crushing, calcining, grinding, and sieving.

steps.description: Diatomite was crushed and calcined at a temperature of 500 °C for 4 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and calcine diatomite at 500 °C for 4 hours

steps.description: The calcined diatomite was then ground and sieved with sieve #200 to have diatomite powder.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and sieve calcined diatomite to obtain powder

methods.description: Procedure for blending diatomite with Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) and determining water demand and setting time.

steps.description: The diatomite was blended with OPC with diatomite content (w/w) of 0 %, 10 %, 20 % and 30 %.
steps.searchable_summary: blend diatomite with OPC at specified weight percentages

steps.description: The water demand and setting time -initial and final -of blended cement paste were determined according to EN 196-3 and ASTM C191-01a.
steps.searchable_summary: determine water demand and setting time using EN 196-3 and ASTM C191-01a

methods.description: Measurement of the flow of normal mortar using a standard flow table according to ASTM standards.

steps.description: The flow of normal mortar was measured according to ASTM C1437-07 and ASTM C230/C230M-08 using the standard flow table.
steps.searchable_summary: measure mortar flow using ASTM C1437-07 and ASTM C230/C230M-08

methods.description: Testing the flexural and compressive strength of cement specimens according to EN and ASTM standards.

steps.description: The flexural and compressive strength measurement was conducted according to EN 196-1 and ASTM C109/C109M-02.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct flexural and compressive strength tests using EN 196-1 and ASTM C109/C109M-02
